updates.summary: This patch release contains two targeted bug fixes with no breaking changes. The fixes address a merge conflict state preservation issue and a stderr/stdout interleaving regression introduced in 16.3.0 when switching to tinyexec. Both fixes improve correctness and stability, making this a low-risk upgrade.
ramifications: Merge conflict status (.git/MERGE_HEAD) will now be correctly preserved when using --hide-unstaged or --hide-all options, which could change behavior for workflows that previously inadvertently lost merge state.

Git commands that succeed (exit code 0) but emit warnings to stderr will no longer treat those warnings as errors, restoring the prior behavior from nano-spawn/execa days and potentially resolving spurious failures in CI pipelines.

updates.summary: This is a low-risk patch release focused entirely on bug fixes for several ESLint rules. The fixes address autofix correctness issues such as comment preservation, parenthesization, and false positives — all of which improve reliability without changing rule logic. No breaking changes are present.
ramifications: Autofixes for `consistent-indexed-object-style` will now correctly preserve comments that were previously being removed.
Autofixes for `no-unnecessary-type-assertion` will now correctly wrap object literals in parentheses when removing TSTypeAssertion in arrow function bodies, preventing potential syntax errors.
The `no-unnecessary-type-assertion` rule will no longer produce false positives for template literal expressions.
The `no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare` autofix had a precedence bug corrected, which may slightly alter the output of autofixed code in edge cases.
The `no-unnecessary-template-expression` rule now correctly handles ECMAScript line terminators, which may surface or suppress lint warnings in files using non-standard line endings.

updates.summary: This is a purely additive minor update that introduces TypeGen-aware type inference for Portable Text components, along with three new utility types (InferComponents, InferStrictComponents, InferValue). There are no breaking changes — existing code continues to work without modification, and the new features are opt-in. The update is low risk and provides significant developer experience improvements for teams using Sanity TypeGen.
ramifications: New generic type inference on the PortableText component may cause TypeScript to report new type errors in existing code if component handlers are typed incorrectly or too loosely relative to the inferred value type.
Projects not using Sanity TypeGen will see no functional change, but may notice slightly different TypeScript hover types on component props.
The three new exported utility types (InferComponents, InferStrictComponents, InferValue) expand the public API surface but do not conflict with any existing exports.

InferStrictComponents is intentionally strict and will cause compile-time errors if a new custom type or mark is added to the schema without a corresponding handler — teams adopting this type should be aware of the enforcement it adds.

updates.summary: This patch release contains two targeted bug fixes: one addressing merge conflict state preservation when using hide options, and another fixing stderr handling regression introduced in 16.3.0. updates.summary: This patch release contains two targeted bug fixes: one addressing merge conflict state preservation when using hide options, and another fixing stderr handling regression introduced in 16.3.0. Both fixes restore previously correct behavior and reduce the risk of unexpected side effects during staged file linting.
ramifications: Teams using --hide-unstaged or --hide-all options during merge conflict resolutions will now correctly retain .git/MERGE_HEAD state, reducing risk of corrupted merge state.
Git commands that emit warnings to stderr but exit with code 0 will no longer cause issues, aligning behavior back to what was expected with execa/nano-spawn.
The stderr fix corrects a regression from v16.3.0 when tinyexec was introduced, meaning interleaved stdout/stderr output problems should be resolved.
